title: Deeg Palace
tagline: Deeg Palace — heritage city of Rajasthan

overview.short: Deeg Palace is a historical monument in Deeg district in the Indian state of Rajasthan built in the 18th century as a luxurious summer resort by the rulers of the Bharatpur State.
overview.description: Deeg Palace is a historical monument in Deeg district in the Indian state of Rajasthan built in the 18th century as a luxurious summer resort by the rulers of the Bharatpur State. It was strategically positioned near Agra and served as a refuge during invasions. It is the only Hindu-style palace in North India and it is a perfect blend of Mughal and Rajput architecture. The palace was in use until the early 1970s and currently it is preserved as a monument by the Government of Rajasthan.

topPlaces.name: Siege of Deeg
topPlaces.description: The siege of Deeg (11–24 December 1804) was a siege of the main fort at Deeg, now in the Bharatpur district of Rajasthan, India, then within the Bharatpur Kingdom. Forces of the British East India Company, led by General Lake, captured the fort from its Marathan defenders.

topPlaces.name: Deeg
topPlaces.description: Deeg is a historical city in the Deeg district of the Indian state of eastern Rajasthan. It is renowned for its architectural heritage, including palaces, forts, gardens, and fountains.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Deeg
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Deeg, fought on 13 November 1804, took place outside Deeg, now in the Bharatpur district of Rajasthan, India. A force of the British East India Company led by Major General John Henry Fraser defeated a Maratha force under Yashwantrao Holkar and a force of Hindu Jats led by Maharaja Ranjit Singh.

topPlaces.name: Bahaj
topPlaces.description: Bahaj is a village in Deeg tehsil of Bharatpur in Rajasthan, India. known for an ancient palaeochannel buried 23 metres under the village, which was discovered by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in 2025.

items.note: The siege of Deeg (11–24 December 1804) was a siege of the main fort at Deeg, now in the Bharatpur district of Rajasthan, India, then within the Bharatpur Kingdom. Forces of the British East India Company, led by General Lake, captured the fort from its Marathan defenders.
Deeg is a historical city in the Deeg district of the Indian state of eastern Rajasthan. It is renowned for its architectural heritage, including palaces, forts, gardens, and fountains.

The Battle of Deeg, fought on 13 November 1804, took place outside Deeg, now in the Bharatpur district of Rajasthan, India. A force of the British East India Company led by Major General John Henry Fraser defeated a Maratha force under Yashwantrao Holkar and a force of Hindu Jats led by Maharaja Ranjit Singh.

Bahaj is a village in Deeg tehsil of Bharatpur in Rajasthan, India. known for an ancient palaeochannel buried 23 metres under the village, which was discovered by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in 2025.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Deeg Palace?
faq.a: The best time to visit Deeg Palace is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Deeg Palace?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Agra Airport (Pandit Deen Dayal Upadhyay) (72 km) and the nearest railway station is Agra Cantt (75 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Deeg Palace?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Deeg Palace at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Deeg Palace?
faq.a: Stays in Deeg Palace start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Deeg Palace?
faq.a: The top attractions are Siege of Deeg, Deeg, Battle of Deeg, Bahaj.
